name: Monaco

auth.notes: REST API uses API key authentication with Authorization: Bearer mks_<token>; keys are created in Monaco Settings > API Keys. MCP uses OAuth 2.0 at https://mcp.monaco.com/mcp, with an API-key bearer-token option documented for Codex/ChatGPT examples.

rateLimits: 100 requests per minute per organization; 429 responses include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, and Retry-After headers. MCP shares the same per-org limit.
pagination: POST /v1/{entity}/list endpoints accept page and page_size in the JSON body; page is 1-indexed, page_size defaults to 50 and maxes at 500. List responses include pagination.page, page_size, total_count, and total_pages.
notes: Official docs now expose agent-readable llms.txt, endpoint markdown pages, OpenAPI 3.1 at https://api.monaco.com/openapi.json, REST authentication, pagination/filtering/sorting, rate limits, and an MCP overview.
REST paths cover accounts, contacts, opportunities, tasks, tags, meetings, sequences, sequence templates, schemas, and /v1/me.
MCP supports read and write/delete tools for Monaco records, uses existing user permissions, and prompts for writes/deletes by default in compatible clients.
Beta notice says API methods, responses, parameters, MCP tools, and MCP schemas may change while Monaco incorporates feedback.
No official CLI or SDK was found.
